title: Human-gate policy
description: The committed, CI-validated active policy governing authenticated human-gate approvals (DEC-0014). The runtime loads the active policy through a trusted fixed boundary; an approval artifact must not choose its policy. The contract pins the ratified Phase 1B.3A posture as constants: Ed25519 signatures verified with Node.js built-in crypto, the versioned domain-separated canonical payload algorithm, single-use nonce replay consumption, default-deny behavior, and independent_reviewer_named fixed to false — the four DEC-0008 independent-review gates stay frozen until a superseding decision names and enrolls an independent reviewer through the documented versioning procedure. Authorization comes from this policy and its referenced authority registry, never from evidence content.

decision_ref.description: the ratified decision this policy encodes; a different policy requires a superseding decision and a new contract version

signature_algorithm.description: pinned: Ed25519 only (DEC-0014); algorithm substitution is schema-invalid

canonicalization_algorithm.description: versioned canonical serialization: code-unit-sorted object keys, no whitespace, strict UTF-8; the signed bytes are UTF-8 of domain_separator + LF + canonical JSON of the payload; sha256 over the same bytes is the payload digest (src/authority/approval-payload.ts, mirrored in contracts/validate.mjs)

domain_separator.description: domain-separation identifier prefixed to the signed bytes and repeated inside the signed payload

authority_registry_ref.description: registry_id of the exact authority registry this policy trusts; the verifier loads it through the same trusted boundary and rejects any other registry

independent_reviewer_named.description: pinned false at 1.7.0: no independent reviewer is formally named or enrolled (DEC-0008), so every gate requiring independent review fails closed; naming one is a superseding decision plus a contract migration, never a config flip

gate_requirements.description: per-gate authorization requirements; keys must exactly equal allowed_gates (semantic layer), and the four DEC-0008 independent-review gates must carry independent_review_required true (semantic layer)

max_approval_ttl_seconds.description: maximum permitted expires_at - issued_at window; longer-lived approvals are rejected

clock_skew_seconds.description: small documented allowance for issued_at arriving marginally ahead of the verifier's injected clock

replay_policy.description: pinned: one approval authorizes exactly one consumption; the nonce is consumed atomically through the immutable receipt store before any authorized result is returned

default_deny.description: pinned: anything not explicitly authorized by this policy and its registry is denied
